name: Check Into Cash
description_short: Check Into Cash is located at 326 Harding Pl #102, Nashville, TN 37211. Google reviewers rate the branch 4.8 stars across 88 reviews. Call +1 615-781-1050.
description_long:
Check Into Cash is one of the oldest and largest short-term lender chains in the United States, having operated for over 30 years in the consumer finance space. The company maintains a network of physical retail locations — including this Ferndale, Michigan store at 749 East 9 Mile Road — alongside an online application platform, giving borrowers multiple ways to access funds. It is state-licensed in Michigan and posts its retail fee schedule publicly.

The company's core lending products include payday loans (up to $600 in Michigan), auto title loans, installment loans, and a flex line of credit. Beyond loans, each store functions as a broader financial services hub: customers can cash checks, pay bills, send money internationally via Western Union, pick up Green Dot Visa prepaid debit cards, file taxes, and purchase money orders — all under one roof.

What sets Check Into Cash apart from purely online lenders is its brick-and-mortar presence. Approved borrowers can walk out of a store the same day with cash in hand, which matters for consumers without bank accounts suited to ACH transfers, or those who need physical documentation support. Store hours are generous on weekdays (open as early as 8am Fridays), and the application requirements are straightforward: government ID, proof of income, and an active checking account.

Check Into Cash serves a real need for consumers who lack access to traditional credit products and face genuine short-term emergencies. However, payday loans carry extremely high APRs — typical in this industry but important to understand. The $600 Michigan payday loan cap is modest, and these products are explicitly designed for short-term gaps, not recurring borrowing. Consumers who roll over or re-borrow frequently will face compounding costs. Title loans carry the additional risk of vehicle repossession if payments are missed.
pros: 30+ years in operation — established, regulated lender with state licensing posted publicly
Payday loans available both in-store and online for flexibility
Same-day in-store cash if approved — no waiting for ACH transfer
Multiple financial services in one location: Western Union, check cashing, bill pay, prepaid debit, tax prep
Extended weekday hours (Mon–Fri until 7pm, Friday opens at 8am)
Straightforward application requirements: ID, checking account, and proof of income
Retail fee schedule publicly accessible on the Michigan state license page
cons: Payday loans carry very high APRs — standard for the product type but costly for borrowers who roll over
Michigan payday loan cap is $600, which may not cover larger unexpected expenses
Requires an active checking account, which may exclude fully unbanked consumers from loan products
Title loans put the borrower's vehicle at risk if repayment is not met
Closed Sundays; Saturday hours limited to 9am–4pm
best_for: Consumers with poor or no credit facing a short-term cash gap before their next paycheck
Michigan residents who need in-person service or same-day cash in hand
People who need bundled financial services (money transfers, check cashing) alongside a small loan
Vehicle owners who need more than the $600 payday loan cap and can use a title loan instead
diagnosis: Check Into Cash is best for banked consumers with limited credit options who need fast cash under $600 and prefer or require in-person service with same-day funding. The main caveat is that payday loan fees are very high — these products are appropriate only for genuine short-term emergencies, and repeated borrowing quickly becomes expensive.
services: Green Dot Visa Prepaid Debit Cards
